subject: Chemistry
options: compound with both alcohol and acid functional groups
solution:
Alpha hydrogen is not present in given carbonyl compound so, Cannizzaro reaction takes place. In this reaction one molecule get oxidised to salt of carboxylic acid and another molecule get reduced to alcohol. Hydrolysis of salt of carboxylic acid will produce carboxylic acid. Reaction can be represented as below:

Clearly, dicarboxylic acid is not produced.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Friedel-Craft acylation reaction in benzene takes place. Aluminium chloride is Lewis acid which helps in generating electrophile as shown in first step of reaction.

This electrophile get substituted in benzene and product (A) is obtained. In next step, Clemmensen's reduction occur which converts aldehyde or ketone into hydrocarbon.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In first step, acetylation of aniline occurs. This process is done to lower the activating nature of amine otherwise poly substitution will occur.

On bromination, bromine group is directed at para position. In ortho position repulsion will dominate due to which its percentage of ortho product is less.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Wet deposition is the acidic water received through rain, fog, snow, dew, mist, etc. Dry deposition is deposition of wind blown acidic gases and particles on the ground. Ammonium salt in rain drop result in wet deposition. Oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur settle down on ground as dry deposition.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
NaOH and C2H5OH is alcoholic NaOH which is used for dehydrohalogenation of alkenes. This beta elimination is anti-elimination and Saytzeff alkene (more stable) is major product. In the given reaction, more stable conjugated alkene is formed as major product.

Note : There is ambiguity in answer given by NTA which is 4.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In first step nitration occurs by mixture of concentrated nitric acid and concentrated sulphuric acid. From this step nitrobenzene (A) is produced.

In next step chlorination of nitrobenzene occurs, nitro group is meta directing which orients the incoming electrophile at meta position which results in formation of compound (B).

In last step, reduction occurs and nitro group is converted into amino group and produces compound (C).

subject: Chemistry
solution: In first step, bromination takes place which reacts with magnesium metal to form Grignard's reagent. This reagent generates very strong base which abstracts acidic hydrogen from methanol to produce benzene.

subject: Chemistry
options: Atomic hydrogen is produced when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> molecules at a high temperature are irradiated with UV radiation.

At around <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2000</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the dissociation of dihydrogen into its atoms is nearly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>%</mo></math>

Bond dissociation enthalpy of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is highest among diatomic gaseous molecules which contain a single bond.

Dihydrogen is produced on reacting zinc with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> as well as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaOH</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>aq</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>.</mo></mrow></msub></math>.
solution: Atomic hydrogen is produced at high temperature in an electric arc or under ultraviolet radiations. The dissociation of dihydrogen at 2000 K is only 0.081%. H–H bond dissociation enthalpy is highest for a single bond for any diatomic molecule. Dihydrogen can be produced on reacting Zn with dil. HCl as well as NaOH (aq.).

solution: lodine oxygen bond is stable due to the greater polarity of bond and stability of chlorine oxygen bond is due to multiple bond formation with orbital of chlorine atom. So, the stability order of oxide is given as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>></mo><mi>Cl</mi><mo>></mo><mi>Br</mi><mo>.</mo></math>

subject: Chemistry
solution: Polyester is any of a group of polymers that consist basically the repeated units of an ester. Polyester is a synthetic fabric that’s usually derived from petroleum. Dacron, PHBV and Glyptal are polyesters. Novolac is a linear condensation polymer of phenol and formaldehyde.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Disproportionation reactions are a special type of redox reactions. One of the reactants in a disproportionation reaction always contains an element that can exist in at least three oxidation states. The element of reacting species is in intermediate oxidation state and simultaneously gets oxidised and reduced. MnO42-,ClO2-,Cl2 and Mn3+ are having intermediate oxidation states.

Note: No option is correct in the actual question. One option is modified for its correctness.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given equations are x2a2+y2 b2=1 ...1 and x2+y2=ab.....2 where  a>b.

From 1,

b2x2+a2y2=a2b2

Substituting value of y2 from 2 in the above equation

b2x2+a2ab-x2=a2b2

⇒b2-a2x2=a2b2-a3b
⇒x2=ba2b-ab2-a2=a2ba+b

∴x=a2ba+b

Substituting value of x2 in 2, we get

y2=ab2a+b

∴y=ab2a+b
Point of intersection is a2ba+b, ab2a+b

Now, from 1,
x2a2+y2b2=1

⇒2xa2+2yb2dydx=0
⇒dydx=m1=-b2xa2y

From 2,
x2+y2=ab

⇒2x+2y·dydx=0

⇒dydx=m2=-xy

Let θ be the angle between curve 1 & 2.
∴tanθ=m1-m21+m1m2=-b2xa2y+xy1+b2x2a2y2=xya2-b2b2x2+a2y2=xya2-b2a2b2

∴tanθa2ba+b, ab2a+b=a3b3(a+b)2·a2-b2a2b2=(a-b)ab

Hence, θ=tan-1a-bab

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given f0=0, f1=1 and f2=2

Let, hx=fx-x

Clearly hx, will be continuous and twice differentiable on 0, 2.

Now, h0=h1=h2=0

By Rolle's mean value theorem in 0, 1, we get

h'c1=0   

⇒f'c1-1=0   

⇒f'c1=1, where c1∈0, 1

Also, in the interval 1, 2

h'c2=0

⇒f׀c2-1=0

⇒f'c2=1, where c2∈1, 2

Now, use Rolle's theorem on c1, c2 for h'x, 

 We have, h''(c)=0

⇒f''(c)=0, where c∈c1, c2

Hence,  f''(c)=0 for some x∈0, 2.

solution:
Given, x+1-2log23+2x+2log410-2-x=0

⇒x+1-2log23+2x+2log2210.2x-12x=0

⇒x+1-2log23+2x+log210.2x-12x=0

⇒x+1-2log23+2x+log210.2x-1-log22x=0

⇒x+1-log23+2x2+log210.2x-1-xlog22=0

⇒x+1-log23+2x2+log210.2x-1-x=0

⇒x+1+log210.2x-13+2x2-x=0

⇒1+log210.2x-13+2x2=0

⇒log210.2x-13+2x2=-1

⇒log210.2x-19+2x2+6.2x=log212

⇒10.2x-19+2x2+6.2x=12

⇒210.2x-1=9+2x2+6.2x

⇒2x2-14.2x+11=0

Let 2x=y

⇒y2-14y+11=0

Let roots are y1=2x1 & y2=2x2

Product of roots, y1y2=2x1×2x2=2x1+x2=11

⇒log2x1+x2=log11

⇒x1+x2log2=log11

⇒x1+x2=log11log2

Hence, x1+x2=log211

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given, α+β+γ=2π

Now, Δ=1cosγcosβcosγ1cosαcosβcosα1

By expanding along the first column, we get

∆=11-cos2α-cosγcosγ-cosα.cosβ+cosβcosα.cosγ-cosβ

∆=1-cos2α-cos2γ+cosγ.cosα.cosβ+cosβ.cosα.cosγ-cos2β

=1-cos2α-cos2β-cos2γ+2cosα.cosβ.cosγ

=sin2α-cos2β-cosγ(cosγ-2cosα.cosβ)

=-cos(α+β).cos(α-β)-cosγcos2π-(α+β)-2cosα.cosβ

=-cos(2π-γ)cos(α-β)-cosγcos(α+β)-2cosα.cosβ

=-cos(2π-γ).cos(α-β)-cosγcosα.cosβ-sinα.sinβ-2cosα.cosβ

=-cosγ.cos(α-β)+cosγ.cos(α-β)

=0

So, ∆=0

Hence, the system of equations has infinitely many solutions.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given:yxdydx=y2x2+ϕy2x2ϕ'y2x2 ....1 

Let yx=t

⇒y=xt

⇒dydx=t+x·dtdx

∴tt+xdtdx=t2+ϕt2ϕ't2

⇒xtdtdx=ϕt2ϕ't2

⇒t·ϕ't2ϕt2dt=1xdx
Integrating both sides

∫t·ϕ't2ϕt2dt=∫1xdx

Let ϕt2=p

⇒ϕ't2.2t=dp

⇒12∫1pdp=∫1xdx

⇒12lnp=lnx+C

⇒12lnϕt2=lnx+C

⇒12lnϕy2x2=lnx+C ...2

If x=1, y=-1 then C=12lnϕ1

Substituting value of C in 2

12lnϕy2x2=lnx+12lnϕ1

⇒lnϕy2x2=lnx2+lnϕ1

If x=2 then 

lnϕy24=ln4+lnϕ1

SO, ϕy24=4ϕ1

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Parametric point on the given ellipse is 2sinθ, 3cosθ

Let, the mid-point of line segments joining -3,-5 and 2sinθ,3cosθ is h, k

Then, by using mid-point formula, we get

2sinθ-32=h, 3cosθ-52=k

⇒2sinθ=2h+3, 3cosθ=2k+5

⇒sinθ=2h+32, cosθ=2k+53

We know, sin2θ+cos2θ=1

⇒2h+322+2k+532=1

⇒144h2+9+12h+194k2+25+20k=1

⇒36h2+16k2+108h+80k+145=0

Hence, the locus of x,y is 36x2+16y2+108x+80y+145=0

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given functional equation is 

fm+n=fm+fn;m,n∈N ....1

Put m=n=3

f(3+3)=f(3)+f(3)

⇒f(3)=9∵f6=18

Put m=2,n=1 in equation 1

∴f(3)=f(2+1)=f(2)+f(1)

=f(1+1)+f(1)

=f(1)+f(1)+f(1)

9=3f(1)

⇒f(1)=3

∴f(2)=f(1+1)=f(1)+f(1)=6

Now,

f(2)·f(3)=(6)(9)=54

solution:
The domain of the function

fx=sin-13x2+x-1(x-1)2+cos-1x-1x+1 

For, cos-1x-1x+1

-1≤x-1x+1≤1

⇒-1≤1-2x+1≤1

⇒-2≤-2x+1≤0

⇒0≤1x+1≤1

⇒x+1∈[1,∞)

⇒x∈[0,∞) ....i

For, sin-13x2+x-1(x-1)2

-1≤3x2+x-1x-12≤1 

⇒-x-12≤3x2+x-1≤x-12, x≠1

Now, -x-12≤3x2+x-1, x≠1

⇒4x2-x≥0 , x≠1

⇒x4x-1≥0, x≠1

⇒x∈(-∞, 0]∪14, ∞ -1  ....ii

And 3x2+x-1≤x-12, x≠1

⇒2x2+3x-2≤0, x≠1

⇒x+22x-1≤0, x≠1

⇒x∈-2, 12  ....iii

Domain of the function sin-13x2+x-1(x-1)2 from the equations ii & iii is

⇒x∈-2, 0∪14, 12 ....iv

Now the domain of the given function will be the intersection of the equation i & iv

Hence, domain is x∈14, 12∪{0}

=∫tdtt3+1=∫t(t+1)t2-t+1dt

A=-13, B=13, C=13

From equation i, Comparing from both sides
⇒α=-13, β=16 and γ=13
So 18α+β+γ2

=3

subject: Mathematics
solution:
The negation of the statement (p∨r)⇒(q∨r) is given by

~p∨r⇒q∨r

≡~~p∨r∨q∨r

≡p∨r∧~q∨r

≡p∨r∧~q∧~r

≡p∨r∧~r∧~q

≡p∧~r∨r∧~r∧~q

≡((p∧~r)∨ϕ)∧(~q)

solution:
The total possible 4 digit numbers are 9×10×10×10=9000

Let, X is the 4 digit number divisible by 3

1002, 1005, ………, 9999

a=1002, d=3 & an=9999

9999=1002+n3-13

n3=3000

Let, Y is the 4 digit numbers divisible by 7

1001,1008, ………, 9996

a=1001, d=7 & an=9996

9996=1001+n7-17

n7=1286

Now, 4 digit numbers divisible by both 3 and 7

1008,1029,...........9996

a=1008, d=21, an=9996

Now, 9996=1008+n-121

n=429

Hence,  4 digit numbers neither divisible by 7 nor 3

=9000-1286-3000+429

=5143

Let a, b, c, d, e be 5 unknown observations.

Given that No. of observation, n=7, Mean =8, Variance =16

∴ sum of observations  =No. of observation ×Mean=7×8=56

⇒ Mean of 5 remaining observations =56-8-65=425

As, we know that,

Variance for 7 observations=Σxi2n-x¯2

⇒16=Σxi27-64

⇒Σxi2=560

⇒a2+b2+c2+d2+e2+64+36=560

⇒a2+b2+c2+d2+e2=460

Variance of remaining 5 observations =Σxi2n-x¯2=4605-4252=53625

∴12αβ1561321=12

⇒αβ1561321=24

⇒α6-2-β5-3+110-18=±24

⇒4α-2β-8=±24

⇒4α-2β=32, 4α-2β+16=0

⇒2α-β-16=0, 2α-β+8=0

So point will be either α,2α+8 , α,2α-16

For the point α,2α+8,

Distance from origin
D=α2+(2α+8)2=5α2+32α+64 ∵β=2α+8

⇒D2=5α2+32α+64

For maximum or minimum length,

dD2dα=10α+32=0

⇒α=-165

∴β=-325+8=85

∴D=-1652+852=855=85

Similarly if β=2α-16, D=α2+2α-162=5α2-64α+256

∴D2=5α2-64α+256

For maximum or minimum length,

dD2dα=0

⇒10α-64=0

∴α=325

∴D=3252+645-162=165 at  α=325
So, least possible length of line segment is 85
